mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in INDPRO signals improving real activity and operating leverage for listed firms, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if walk-forward tests show positive 3-month INDPRO trends are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in no more than 50% of cases or produce Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, the higher short-rate anchor raises equity discount rates and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases or an average return not below the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPIAUCSL while FEDFUNDS is already above 4% signals persistent inflation pressure that forces restrictive policy to remain in place, lowering equity valuations over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS is above 4%
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy with inflation persistence
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not underperform unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns by at least 25 basis points on average in walk-forward tests or if the interaction term has the wrong sign in more than half of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Low UNRATE combined with continued PAYEMS expansion signals resilient household income and stable consumption demand, supporting corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is below 4% and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in less than 50% of cases or if average triggered returns are below the unconditional 20-day average in two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals weakening interest-sensitive demand and future construction activity, reducing cyclical earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST declines greater than 10% over 3 months are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if triggered average returns exceed the unconditional average.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in RSAFS signals strengthening consumer demand, improving revenue momentum for equity issuers and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS trends do not generate a positive 20-day return hit rate above 52% in walk-forward tests or if Rank IC is below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A one-month CPIAUCSL increase above 0.3% during a high FEDFUNDS regime triggers near-term rate-risk repricing, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 1-month growth exceeds 0.3% and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative hit rate is below 55% across the full walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising GDPC1 together with rising PAYEMS signals broad real-demand expansion, translating into stronger earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter change is positive and PAYEMS 3-month change is positive
failure_condition: Reject if the joint positive GDPC1 and PAYEMS condition has a 60-day S&P 500 average return below the unconditional average or a positive-return hit rate below 52% in walk-forward tests.
